tasks.completion_notes: Phase 2 e2e gate test in tests/integration/test_phase2_e2e.py — 3 focused tests against a session-scoped testcontainers Postgres reuse the shared db_pool / postgres_dsn fixtures from tests/conftest.py (pytestmark = DOCKER_REQUIRED for clean Docker-less skips). Test A (test_phase2_e2e_emits_claim_start_complete_per_task) imports a 3-task plan via `whilly plan import`, plays one worker manually through TaskRepository.claim_task → start_task → complete_task for every task, then asserts the events table holds the full CLAIM/START/COMPLETE trio per task ordered by created_at, with payload version increments 1/2/3 and the CLAIM payload pinning worker_id. Test B (test_phase2_e2e_export_after_completion_shows_done) runs the same loop and asserts `whilly plan export` stdout shows status=DONE for every task plus version=3 (pin counter so a refactor that flips status without bumping version cannot pass silently). Test C (test_phase2_e2e_tasks_table_consistent_with_export) is a belt-and-braces cross-check: same loop, then SELECT directly from tasks vs. exported JSON — divergence on status/version means the SELECT path in _select_plan_with_tasks reads a stale projection. CLI handler invocation goes through asyncio.to_thread because run_plan_command internally calls asyncio.run, which Python refuses to nest under the test loop spawned by pytest-asyncio auto-mode; routing through to_thread parks the handler on a worker thread that owns its own loop and keeps capsys capture intact (verified — tests parse JSON from captured stdout). worker rows are seeded directly via raw SQL on the borrowed pool connection (same pattern as test_concurrent_claims._seed_workers) so this test has no dependency on the transport layer (TASK-021b). _drive_one_task_to_done helper threads version through claim → start → complete; a regression where any method drops the version bump surfaces as VersionConflictError from the next call before assertions even reach the database. Verification: ruff check + ruff format --check whilly/ tests/ clean (172 files); pytest tests/integration/test_phase2_e2e.py -v → 3 passed in 2.0s; full pytest -q → 1160 passed, 1 pre-existing CLAUDE_BIN env-leak failure in test_agent_backend_claude (unrelated, documented in TASK-022c notes — passes with env -u CLAUDE_BIN). Unblocks: TASK-016 (Phase 3 integration test) gains a working Phase 2 baseline to compose on top of.

tasks.completion_notes: whilly/worker/local.py implements run_local_worker(repo, runner, plan, worker_id, *, idle_wait, max_iterations) — async loop claim_task → start_task → run agent → complete_task | fail_task. Returns frozen WorkerStats (iterations, completed, failed, idle_polls). When claim_task returns None: increments idle_polls and asyncio.sleep(idle_wait). VersionConflictError at start/complete/fail is logged and the loop continues — abandoning the row is the safe race-loss outcome. FAIL reason carries exit_code + truncated stdout (cap 500 chars + ellipsis) so audit rows stay bounded. Required adding TaskRepository.start_task (CLAIMED → IN_PROGRESS) so complete_task SQL filter passes. 17 unit tests in tests/unit/test_local_worker.py cover happy path, fail paths, idle path, version conflicts, version threading, and reason-truncation. mypy --strict whilly/worker/local.py passes. Production callers loop until cancelled (SIGTERM lands in TASK-019b2); tests pass max_iterations=N to terminate.

tasks.completion_notes: whilly/worker/main.py adds run_worker(repo, runner, plan, worker_id, *, idle_wait, heartbeat_interval, max_iterations) — composes whilly.worker.local.run_local_worker with run_heartbeat_loop under one asyncio.TaskGroup. DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = 30.0 per PRD FR-1.6. Coordination uses an asyncio.Event ('stop') rather than task.cancel(): the inner closure _worker_then_stop sets stop in its finally block on any exit path (max_iterations reached, exception, future SIGTERM in TASK-019b2), and the heartbeat coroutine wakes from asyncio.wait_for(stop.wait(), timeout=interval) and returns normally — TaskGroup unwinds without CancelledError plumbing. First heartbeat tick fires immediately on entry (no one-interval gap where the visibility-timeout sweep could mistake a brand-new worker for a stale one). update_heartbeat exceptions are caught and logged at WARNING — heartbeat is best-effort liveness, never kills the worker. CancelledError is not caught (inherits BaseException) so structured cancellation still works for TASK-019b2. Required adding TaskRepository.update_heartbeat(worker_id) -> bool: single UPDATE workers SET last_heartbeat = NOW() WHERE worker_id = $1, no transaction wrapper / no audit event (heartbeats fire every 30s × N workers, would bloat events without audit value). Returns False when row missing (admin revoked, FK cascade) so the heartbeat loop can log+continue without exception coupling. Tests: 5 unit tests in tests/unit/test_worker_main.py (FakeRepo duck-types TaskRepository, covers default constant pin, heartbeat ticks during idle loop, transient update_heartbeat exception → logged + worker continues, heartbeat terminates when max_iterations reached even with long heartbeat_interval, inner-loop exceptions surface through TaskGroup as ExceptionGroup with original RuntimeError preserved). 4 integration tests in tests/integration/test_worker_heartbeat.py with testcontainers Postgres (direct repo update_heartbeat smoke + advances column, returns False for unknown worker, end-to-end run_worker advances workers.last_heartbeat against backdated baseline, TaskGroup terminates within 5s even with heartbeat_interval=30s). tasks.notes: Implemented whilly/adapters/transport/auth.py with two FastAPI Depends factories: make_bearer_auth(expected_token) gates per-worker RPCs (claim/complete/fail/heartbeat) against WHILLY_WORKER_TOKEN, make_bootstrap_auth(expected_token) gates POST /workers/register against WHILLY_WORKER_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN. The split is intentional (PRD FR-1.2): rotating the bootstrap secret locks out new workers without invalidating already-issued bearer tokens, and vice versa. Token comparison uses secrets.compare_digest to close the timing-attack channel — even on a shared static token, '==' would short-circuit on the first mismatched byte and leak token contents over enough probes. Bearer extraction is case-insensitive on the scheme (RFC 7235 §2.1) but case-sensitive on the token (RFC 6750 §2.1); empty tokens after the prefix and non-Bearer schemes (Basic/Digest) raise 401 before reaching compare_digest so an accidentally-empty server-side env can't conspire with an empty Authorization to authenticate as 'anyone'. 401 responses carry WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="whilly" per RFC 6750 §3 — FastAPI's default HTTPException doesn't set it, so _bearer_401() builds the header explicitly. Module-level lazy shims bearer_auth/bootstrap_auth read the env on first call and cache the closure — production code uses make_*_auth() in create_app() (TASK-021a3) so missing-env errors fail-fast at startup, but the shims keep ad-hoc routes ergonomic. reset_lazy_dependencies() exists for tests that mutate env across cases. tasks.completion_notes: RemoteWorkerClient gained two RPC methods on top of the TASK-022a1 _request primitive. register(hostname) -> RegisterResponse calls POST /workers/register with bootstrap=True (swaps in self._bootstrap_token via the existing seam) and validates the response via RegisterResponse.model_validate; heartbeat(worker_id) -> HeartbeatResponse calls POST /workers/{worker_id}/heartbeat using the per-worker bearer that's already pinned on the long-lived httpx.AsyncClient. Both methods build their request bodies through the pydantic schemas (RegisterRequest, HeartbeatRequest) so an empty hostname / empty worker_id is rejected at the schema layer (min_length=1) BEFORE any wire call — programmer errors stay at the right tier and don't surface as a wire-level 422 the operator has to read in a server log. Centralised response parsing in a new _parse_response[T: BaseModel] static method: a 2xx body that doesn't match the schema (extra=forbid violation, missing required field, version skew between worker and control plane) becomes ServerError with the underlying pydantic.ValidationError preserved as __cause__; a 2xx with non-JSON content (captive portal, broken proxy) likewise becomes ServerError. Both cases are server-side protocol failures, not retryable transient faults — the same exception class as 'exhausted retry budget' lets the worker's outer loop in TASK-022b2 keep its classifier simple. Design choice that's pinned by tests: register does NOT mutate self._token. RemoteWorkerStats frozen dataclass mirrors WorkerStats minus released_on_shutdown (shutdown is owned by TASK-022b3, not 022b1 — same slicing as 019a → 019b1 → 019b2 on the local side). Loop contract: (1) claim returns Task | None; None means server-side long-poll budget expired, re-poll IMMEDIATELY (no client-side sleep — the ACs load-bearing 204 path; a sleep here would double the budget on the server and burn worker capacity). (2) runner builds prompt via whilly.core.prompts.build_task_prompt — same projection as the local worker. (3) Outcome routing: is_complete && exit_code==0 → client.complete(task.id, worker_id, task.version); else → client.fail(...) with reason exit_code=<n>: <truncated stdout> (cap 500 chars matching local._FAIL_REASON_OUTPUT_CAP so dashboards aggregate both flavours under one prefix). VersionConflictError on either RPC is logged with structured fields (defensive actual_status.value if exc.actual_status else None ternary handles the row-gone case) and the loop continues — same abandon-and-re-poll policy as the local worker (PRD FR-2.4 race against peer / sweep). Documented protocol gap in module docstring: server complete_task SQL filters status=IN_PROGRESS but theres no HTTP /tasks/{id}/start endpoint (TASK-022a2/a3 shipped only register/heartbeat/claim/complete/fail per scope), so a real run against the production server would surface 409 with actual_status=CLAIMED until a future task adds /start (or relaxes the complete filter); TASK-022b1s scope is the loop *shape*, unit-tested against a stub client. Bare loop per AC: no heartbeat (022b2 owns), no signals (022b3 owns) — exit paths are max_iterations (test-only) and outer CancelledError (production fallback once supervisor lands). 16 unit tests in tests/unit/test_remote_worker.py: happy path (claim→run→complete bumps completed, version threads through), failure path (non-zero exit AND missing completion marker both route to fail), idle/204 (re-polls without asyncio.sleep — fake_sleep fixture pins this as an empty list), version conflict on complete and on fail (logged at WARNING and loop continues), conflict with actual_status=None (defensive log-format ternary), max_iterations=0 boundary, plus the four pure-helper tests for _truncate_output / _build_fail_reason that mirror locals coverage. Verification: ruff check + format clean, mypy --strict whilly/worker/remote.py clean (and the whole whilly/worker/ package — 4 files), import-linter KEPT (whilly.core stays I/O-free), pytest tests/unit/test_remote_worker.py = 16/16, full pytest -q = 1113 passed (1 unrelated CLAUDE_BIN env-leak failure documented in TASK-019b1/022a2 notes). Re-exported run_remote_worker, RemoteWorkerStats, RemoteRunnerCallable from whilly.worker.__init__ alongside the existing local entry points. Unblocks TASK-022b2 (heartbeat parallel task under asyncio.TaskGroup over this loop) and TASK-022b3 (SIGTERM/SIGINT graceful shutdown, mirroring 019b2s stop-event pattern).

tasks.completion_notes: Added two layered entry points to whilly/worker/remote.py: (1) run_remote_heartbeat_loop(client, worker_id, *, interval, stop) — mirrors whilly.worker.main.run_heartbeat_loop, but the state-mutating call goes through RemoteWorkerClient.heartbeat (HTTP) instead of TaskRepository.update_heartbeat (SQL). The first tick fires immediately on entry (no one-interval gap where the visibility-timeout sweep could mistake a brand-new worker for a stale one); subsequent waits use asyncio.wait_for(stop.wait(), timeout=interval) so a graceful shutdown wakes up without waiting out the full interval. Two-tier except clause — typed HTTPClientError first (auth / server / generic 4xx — log at WARNING), then a generic Exception catch-all for httpx-level / asyncio-level oddities. CancelledError bypasses both (BaseException) so structured cancellation still works. ok=False from the server (recoverable 'unknown worker_id' branch documented on HeartbeatResponse) is logged at INFO rather than WARNING so a misconfigured worker doesn't drown the journal at WARNING for every tick — the supervisor (TASK-022b3 + future re-register flow) is the one expected to act. (2) run_remote_worker_with_heartbeat(client, runner, plan, worker_id, *, heartbeat_interval, max_iterations, stop) — composition root mirroring whilly.worker.main.run_worker. Both coroutines run under one asyncio.TaskGroup; when the main loop exits the inner closure _worker_then_stop's finally: stop.set() wakes the heartbeat from its wait_for and the TaskGroup unwinds without CancelledError plumbing. Pattern: stop event > task.cancel() because the latter surfaces a CancelledError that TaskGroup treats as propagatable; the stop-event approach lets the heartbeat exit normally. DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL = 30.0 added to remote.py — same value as whilly.worker.main's, pinned by a parity test (test_default_heartbeat_interval_matches_local_worker_constant). Asymmetric to local side: heartbeat composition lives IN remote.py (not a separate remote_main.py) since the AC's key_files only lists remote.py and TASK-022b3 will land in the same file. whilly/worker/__init__.py re-exports the new symbols (DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL collides with main's but both are 30.0; second-import wins, harmless). 9 integration tests in tests/integration/test_remote_worker_heartbeat.py with hand-rolled FakeRemoteClient (no testcontainers — the remote worker has no direct DB; the unit-of-integration is the TaskGroup composition itself). Coverage: parity constant, idle-run heartbeat ticking, transient HTTPClientError survival + WARNING log, ok=False recoverable branch + INFO log, long-interval shutdown via wait_for collapsing to immediate exit on max_iterations, inner-loop exception propagation through the TaskGroup as ExceptionGroup, externally-supplied pre-set stop event terminating both tasks before any tick, generic Exception catch-all (RuntimeError) survival + WARNING log, parallel ticking during a slow runner (>=2 ticks during a 50ms task with 1ms heartbeat). Verification: ruff check + format clean; mypy --strict whilly/worker/remote.py = clean; lint-imports KEPT (whilly.core stays I/O-free, no asyncpg/httpx/fastapi/uvicorn/alembic leakage); pytest tests/integration/test_remote_worker_heartbeat.py = 9/9; full pytest -q = 1123 passed (1113 baseline + 9 new + 1 new parity test in heartbeat file = 1123). Unblocks TASK-022b3 (SIGTERM/SIGINT plumbing on top of run_remote_worker_with_heartbeat — same pattern as 019b2 layering on 019b1).

tasks.completion_notes: Implemented SIGTERM/SIGINT graceful shutdown for the remote worker mirroring TASK-019b2 on the local side, but with the release primitive surfaced as a new HTTP RPC instead of a direct SQL call (the remote worker has no asyncpg path — PRD SC-6). Three layers of changes: (1) New /tasks/{task_id}/release endpoint added to whilly/adapters/transport/server.py — wraps TaskRepository.release_task, projects 409 through the existing _conflict_response helper (whose docstring already anticipated 'a future release_task HTTP endpoint will reuse it'). New ReleaseRequest/ReleaseResponse pydantic schemas in whilly/adapters/transport/schemas.py with the same shape as FailRequest/FailResponse (worker_id + version + non-empty reason) so the wire surface is symmetric. (2) New RemoteWorkerClient.release(task_id, worker_id, version, reason) method in whilly/adapters/transport/client.py + release_path() helper colocated with complete_path/fail_path. Same retry/auth/4xx mapping as complete and fail — a 409 with actual_status=PENDING is the canonical 'sweep beat us to it' idempotent-success branch the worker checks for. (3) whilly/worker/remote.py now wires the full signal chain: SHUTDOWN_RELEASE_REASON='shutdown' constant (mirrors whilly.worker.local.SHUTDOWN_RELEASE_REASON so dashboards aggregate both flavours under one prefix), _SHUTDOWN_SIGNALS=(SIGTERM,SIGINT), _install_signal_handlers / _remove_signal_handlers / _make_shutdown_handler trio mirroring whilly.worker.main exactly (same NotImplementedError defensive-degradation for Windows ProactorEventLoop and non-main threads, same DEBUG-on-cleanup-error policy so a failed unwind doesn't mask the real exception). _await_runner_or_stop helper duplicated from local.py (the underscore-private name there is intentionally not shared; six lines is cheaper than promoting a public helper that adds no value to non-worker callers). run_remote_worker grew a stop: asyncio.Event | None parameter — when set on entry the loop exits at the iteration boundary; when set during the runner call _await_runner_or_stop cancels the runner and the loop calls client.release with reason='shutdown' before breaking. RemoteWorkerStats grew released_on_shutdown counter so the WorkerStats / RemoteWorkerStats dashboard surface stays symmetric. run_remote_worker_with_heartbeat grew install_signal_handlers=True parameter (defaults to True for production, False for tests so pytest's own SIGINT handler isn't replaced); installs handlers before the TaskGroup enters and removes them in a try/finally on exit so subsequent test runs aren't poisoned. Tests: 4 new integration tests in tests/integration/test_remote_worker_signals.py using a hand-rolled FakeRemoteClient and real os.kill(os.getpid(), sig) — pins the SIGTERM and SIGINT paths through the same _drive_shutdown_via_signal helper, plus a handler-cleanup trip-wire and an install_signal_handlers=False sentinel-survival test. 6 new server-side integration tests in tests/integration/test_transport_tasks.py covering the new /tasks/{id}/release endpoint: CLAIMED→PENDING happy path with audit-row inspection, IN_PROGRESS→PENDING happy path, stale-version 409 with envelope, no-bearer 401, bootstrap-token 401 (PRD FR-1.2 split), empty-reason 422. Verification: ruff check + format clean (169 files); mypy --strict whilly/worker/ + whilly/adapters/transport/ = 9 source files clean; lint-imports --config .importlinter = 1 contract KEPT (SC-6 intact, no asyncpg/fastapi leakage into whilly.core or whilly.worker.remote); pytest tests/integration/test_remote_worker_signals.py = 4/4; pytest tests/unit/ = 386/386; full pytest -q = 1132 passed + 1 pre-existing unrelated test_agent_backend_claude::test_basic_shape failure (CLAUDE_BIN env-leak documented in 019b1 / 022a2 / 022b1 / 022b2 progress notes — passes with env -u CLAUDE_BIN). Unblocks TASK-022c (whilly-worker CLI on top of run_remote_worker_with_heartbeat — entry point is now feature-complete with heartbeat + signal handling + graceful shutdown all wired).

tasks.completion_notes: Console script `whilly-worker = whilly.cli.worker:main` registered in pyproject.toml [project.scripts] (separate binary so the worker process never pays the asyncpg/FastAPI import cost — same isolation the .importlinter contract pins for whilly.core). CLI parses --connect/--token/--plan with CLI > env > error precedence (env vars: WHILLY_CONTROL_URL, WHILLY_WORKER_TOKEN, WHILLY_PLAN_ID) and resolves worker_id with CLI > env > auto-generated <hostname>-<8-hex> (mirrors whilly.cli.run). Composition root opens a RemoteWorkerClient inside `async with` and drives run_remote_worker_with_heartbeat — registration is assumed to have happened out-of-band (no --bootstrap-token here on purpose; mixing flows would defeat the FR-1.2 token split). --once is wired by adding a new `max_processed: int | None = None` parameter to run_remote_worker and run_remote_worker_with_heartbeat — symmetric with the existing max_iterations cap; the CLI sets it to 1. Idle 204 polls and 409 lost-races deliberately do NOT count against max_processed, so a --once worker fights through an empty queue or version skew until it owns a real terminal status. Diagnostics on missing inputs name the env var so a fresh user can fix without grepping source. Tests: 18 new unit tests in test_cli_worker.py (argparse surface, env precedence, missing-required diagnostics, --once translation, worker-id resolution, runner injection, asyncio bridging, console-script entry); 5 new tests in test_remote_worker.py for the max_processed contract (one-completed, one-failed, idle-poll-skips, 409-skips, None-uncapped). tasks.completion_note: Added migration 002_workers_status (status text column with CHECK constraint and partial index on online rows). New TaskRepository.release_offline_workers method runs a single three-CTE statement that flips stale-heartbeat workers to offline, releases their CLAIMED/IN_PROGRESS tasks back to PENDING, and writes one RELEASE event per released task with reason=worker_offline. update_heartbeat now also sets status=online so a recovered worker rejoins transparently. FastAPI lifespan TaskGroup supervises a second coroutine _offline_worker_sweep_loop sharing the existing sweep_stop event; new create_app kwargs heartbeat_timeout_seconds (default 120s) and offline_worker_sweep_interval_seconds (default 30s) with construction-time validation. tests/integration/test_worker_offline.py covers happy path, fresh-heartbeat skip, idempotency on already-offline workers, heartbeat resurrection, and construction-time validation. ruff + mypy --strict (core + adapters) pass; full pytest suite passes (1173/1173 once unrelated CLAUDE_BIN env override is normalized).
